customer_anxiety_profile: Davis is a post-stroke homeowner on a fixed or constrained income, enrolled in a debt-relief program, with active financial anxiety. He is cooperative and warm but self-conscious about money and physical limitations. He responds well to human connection (dog moment) but will disengage if he feels judged or dismissed.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score self-reported at 450 from a debt-relief context — reliability is uncertain; Davis hedged with 'I think it is.' A pulled score may differ materially.
Roof replacement is planned but not scheduled — Davis wants the roof done before solar. Timing is unclear.
Tree removal is in progress but not complete — Davis just got permission five minutes before the call. Physical limitations (stroke) mean this will take time.
Financial anxiety was not addressed — Davis expressed concern about affordability twice and was not reassured or redirected.
sensitive_topics_raised: Stroke three years ago — physical limitations affect pace and capability
Enrolled in National Debt Relief program — financial distress context
Financial anxiety expressed with profanity — 'I'm not fucking wealthy like some people think they are'
exact_promises_rep_made: Parker promised to call Davis back in 'a couple of months' to check if his credit score has improved — no locked date was set.
closer_should_know: The credit DQ is based on a self-reported score of 450 from a debt-relief context — Davis hedged the number. Before treating this as a hard DQ, the callback rep should probe whether a pulled score would differ. More immediately: Davis has a roof replacement planned and Ion does roofing in-house. The roofing bridge was never offered. The next touch should lead with the roofing conversation, which does not require the same credit qualification as solar, and use that relationship to stay in the pipeline for the solar install once the credit situation resolves.
